examples: The spoken word is immediate but lacks permanence.
Much of the world's great architecture has been constructed of stone because of its beauty, permanence, and availability.
When new or difficult material is presented, almost all listeners are faced with a challenge, because human's speech lacks the stability and permanence of the printed word.
What would happen is that effective power would shift, automatically, to those with some permanence.
That is, the ephemeral nature of all speech is being given the permanence of text.
